codeReview.summary: The fix commit fully addresses the prior blocking findings: API-key scope checks are now enforced on all annotation CRUD handlers (health:read for GET, health:write for POST/PATCH/DELETE), and viewer GET date ranges are clamped via enforcePermissions() before querying. The added integration tests cover negative and positive scope cases plus clamp/no-overlap/partial-overlap viewer scenarios. No regressions were identified in owner behavior or response/audit shape.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill requires test-first TDD, but this fix session applied implementation edits before adding new tests while handoff skill feedback still reports followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: apps/web/.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:36 requires "Write tests first (TDD)"; worker transcript skeleton (worker-transcripts.jsonl:21, session 0e714780-ed9c-47f9-b47c-b46015bfa3aa) shows route edits before the later "Now let me add tests" step; handoff marks "followedProcedure": true with no deviations (handoffs/2026-04-05T06-42-22-432Z__fix-annotation-auth-hardening__0e714780-ed9c-47f9-b47c-b46015bfa3aa.json:88-89).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission AGENTS auth guidance is too generic for API data endpoints: it documents getResolvedContext + role checks, but not API-key scope enforcement and viewer date-window clamping, which were the exact prior blocking defects.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/weae1504/.factory/missions/db9d835a-f2b6-43c1-b3f9-be45e7a2a25a/AGENTS.md:24 only states role-check auth guidance; prior fail review identified missing scope/clamp in annotation-crud-api, and this fix adds that logic in apps/web/src/app/api/annotations/route.ts and apps/web/src/app/api/annotations/[id]/route.ts (commit e2b3253).

summary: Pass. Reviewed the prior failure report, original failed commit (0fde193), fix commit (e2b3253), handoff, and transcript skeleton. All previously blocking authorization issues are resolved and no new regressions were found.
